Who works at SPL? Who are the researchers at SPL? The SPL team includes faculty directors Dr. Triantis and Dr. Godfrey, postdoctoral associates Leon Sobrie and Maria Tomai, and PhD students Tatiana Daychman, Yasmin Mashayekhy Fard, Juan Carlos Franco R., and Ibrahim Oguz Cetinkaya. Visit https://www.spl.ise.vt.edu/about/people.html for full profiles.

What is the mission of the System Performance Lab (SPL)? The mission of the lab is to support ISE academic programs and project sponsors with ongoing research and professional services that advance understanding and knowledge of System Performance and contribute to the enabling technology available for design, test, operation, and management of high-performance complex systems.
What type of research does the SPL conduct? The SPL conducts research in System Performance and enables technology for design, test, operation, and management of high-performance complex systems.
What kind of services does the SPL offer? The SPL offers ongoing research and professional services to support ISE academic programs and project sponsors.
What is the focus area of the SPL? The focus area of the SPL is Human-Technology Interactions in Systems Design.
What kind of systems does the SPL work with? The SPL works with high-performance complex systems.

What is the goal of the SPL? The goal is to provide a quality of service to sponsors, students, and faculty that result in the contribution of knowledge formation, technology advancement, and economic development throughout the Commonwealth of Virginia, and beyond.
Who does the SPL aim to provide a quality of service to? The SPL aims to provide a quality of service to sponsors, students, and faculty.
What kind of development does the SPL aim to contribute to? The SPL aims to contribute to knowledge formation, technology advancement, and economic development.
Where does the SPL aim to contribute to development? The SPL aims to contribute to development throughout the Commonwealth of Virginia, and beyond.
What kind of impact does the SPL aim to have? The SPL aims to have an impact on knowledge formation, technology advancement, and economic development.

What research activities are conducted by the SPL? Research activities in the SPL include: Enterprise Performance Measurement (Metrics and Improvement), Data Envelopment Analysis, Fuzzy Sets and Logic, System Dynamics Modeling, Design of Quality Management Systems, Engineering Administration, Systems Engineering Management and Systems Science, Influential Observation Identification, Process Definition, Performance Analysis and Re-engineering, Infrastructure Systems (Resilience and Sustainability), and Evaluation and Assessment of R&D Enterprises.
What is the SPL's current NSF-funded grant amount? $2M
How many peer-reviewed publications has the SPL had? 80+
How many PhD graduates has the SPL produced between 2020 and 2025? 7
What is the date of the 2026 UVA Postdoctoral Research Symposium? April 27, 2026
What department is the SPL group located in? The Grado Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
Where is the SPL group located? Virginia Tech
What does SPL study? How people, technology, and organizations interact to optimize the performance of complex systems
What methods do you use to study technology-intensive systems? We use a combination of quantitative and qualitative methods.
What fields of study inform your research? Industrial and systems engineering, computer science, and organizational studies.
What types of systems do you study? Technology-intensive systems, such as transportation systems and healthcare systems.

What is the topic of the ongoing research mentioned on the SPL website? automation in safety-critical systems
What kind of problems do organizations face? Not all organizational problems are simple puzzles with fixed structures and predictable processes.
What causes today's organizational challenges? Today's challenges arise from dynamic interactions among organizational structures, evolving procedures, and shifting outcomes.
Are all organizational problems predictable? No, not all organizational problems are simple puzzles with fixed structures and predictable processes.
What kind of research is being conducted by SPL? Our research offers actionable guidance for navigating complex challenges and shaping adaptive policies in an era of rapid digital transformation.
Who is SPL partnering with for this research? We are partnering with Human Factors specialists from Clemson University.
What kind of experiments have been conducted by the Human Factors specialists? They have conducted rigorous eye tracking and mental workload experiments.

What is the goal of the research model being developed? By connecting situational awareness to action execution, our model quantifies its impact on efficiency, safety, and operator workload guiding design improvements that optimize real world outcomes.

What does the research model map out? It maps out work system elements, their interrelated processes (including how knowledge is acquired and shared), and the emergence of distributed situational awareness.

What is the focus of the research partnership between SPL and Georgetown University and Brigham Young University? The research partnership focuses on how both organizations and individuals learn from automation use in high stakes environments.
What kind of environments are being studied in this research? High stakes environments are being studied in this research.
What is the goal of the research partnership? The goal of the research partnership is to investigate how both organizations and individuals learn from automation use.
What does the SPL research examine? The SPL research examines macro level organizational learning processes alongside micro level user experience and behavior.
What is being used as a real-world testbed for the research? Infrabel's traffic control centers are being used as a real-world testbed for the research.
What is the nerve center of Belgium's railway infrastructure? Infrabel's traffic control centers are the nerve center of Belgium's railway infrastructure.
What is the goal of the SPL's work? The goal is to drive safer, more efficient, and more resilient rail operations.
What approach does SPL use to model the underlying dynamics in rail operations? SPL uses system dynamics modeling.
What framework does SPL leverage for modeling safety, economic, and workload boundaries? SPL uses Rasmussen's framework.
What methods does SPL use to explore trade-offs between different boundaries? SPL uses causal loop diagrams to explore the key trade-offs between different boundaries.

What are some of the trade-offs SPL considers when evaluating boundaries? Some of the trade-offs SPL considers include intuition versus automation, team familiarity versus flexibility, generalization versus specialization of roles, and competing goal hierarchies.
How do competing goal hierarchies affect individual behavior and organizational outcomes? Competing goal hierarchies shape both individual behavior and organizational outcomes.
What is the research project about? The research project is about understanding complexity in engineering, studying how undergraduate students and professionals recognize and manage complex systems.
Who is the principal investigator (PI) of the research project? The principal investigator (PI) of the research project is Triantis.

What is the central hypothesis of the research project? The central hypothesis is that education in engineering programs and real-world experiences influence engineers' understanding of complex systems.
What is the source of funding for the research project? The research project is funded by the National Science Foundation.
What disciplines will be involved in the research project? The research project is multi-disciplinary and lies at the intersection of complex systems and engineering education.
What types of research questions are being investigated? Four specific research questions (RQ1-RQ4) are being investigated.
How will the research questions be studied? Three vignettes with different levels of structuredness and complexity will be used to represent complex tasks.

What is the relationship between education/expertise and performance on complex tasks? The relationship between engineering students/professionals' level of education/expertise and their performance on three vignettes that vary with respect to problem structuredness and complexity will be studied (RQ1).

How do perceptions of systems thinking competencies relate to performance on complex tasks? The relationship between engineering students/professionals' perceptions of their own systems thinking competencies and their performance on three vignettes that vary with respect to problem structuredness and complexity will be studied (RQ2).

How do engineering students and professionals differ in their approaches to solving complex problems? Engineering students and professionals differ in their approaches to solving problems that vary with respect to problem structuredness and complexity (RQ3).
What is the focus of the research project mentioned on the SPL website? Modeling mental workload and multi level factors that collectively create errors in complex, safety critical systems.
Which institute is involved in the research project? Institute for Society, Culture and Environment (ISCE)-VT
Who is the Principal Investigator (PI) of the research project? Niyousha Hosseinichimeh
Who is the Co-Principal Investigator (Co-PI) of the research project? K.
What type of systems are being studied in the research project? Complex, safety critical systems
Who are the collaborators of Triantis? The collaborators of Triantis are Andrea K. Wittenborn, Robin L. Dillon-Merrill, Roets Bart, and Peter Madsen.

What are the affiliations of Andrea K. Wittenborn? Andrea K. Wittenborn is an Associate Professor at Michigan State University, affiliated with Psychiatry and Behavioral Medicine and Human Development and Family Studies.
What is the affiliation of Peter Madsen? Peter Madsen is affiliated with the School of Business at Brigham Young University.
What is the affiliation of Roets Bart? Roets Bart is the Chief Engineer at INFRABEL Belgian Railway Company.
What is the affiliation of Robin L. Dillon-Merrill? Robin L. Dillon-Merrill is a Professor at Georgetown University, affiliated with the McDonough School of Business.

What are some examples of catastrophic events caused by human errors in safety critical systems? Examples include the 2010 Halle train collision in Belgium that killed 19 people and injured 171 passengers.
How many people were killed in the 2010 Halle train collision? 19 people were killed in the 2010 Halle train collision.
How many passengers were injured in the 2010 Halle train collision? 171 passengers were injured in the 2010 Halle train collision.
What are some of the factors that contribute to human errors? Mental workload, stress, fatigue, mental health, and organizational factors are some of the factors that contribute to human errors.
What is the main goal of this research? The main goal of this research is to investigate dynamic hypotheses about the relationships among the factors that contribute to human errors.
What kind of relationships is this research trying to map? This research aims to map the relationships among mental workload, stress, fatigue, mental health, and organizational factors.
What type of model will be created to analyze the Belgian Railway Company's data? A system dynamics simulation model will be created.
Who will provide the data for the system dynamics simulation model? The data will be provided by the INFRABEL (Belgian Railway Company).
What will be done with the system dynamics simulation model after it's built and tested? The model will be used to run what if analyses and explore interventions.
What is the purpose of this research? This research will suggest the implications of specific interventions that can reduce human errors and stress in the workplace for managers and policymakers.
What is the focus of the Collaborative Research project? The Collaborative Research project focuses on Multi-Perspective Evacuation Performance Measurement.
Who is the PI of the Collaborative Research project? The PI of the Collaborative Research project is Triantis.
What is the program of the National Science Foundation that funded the Collaborative Research project? The program is Infrastructure Management and Extreme Event Program.
What are the consequences of performing well in an evacuation? The consequences of performing well in an evacuation are many.

What are the main goals of utilizing scarce resources? They range from utilizing scarce resources, to saving lives and protecting property, to securing politically viable decisions when a disaster occurs.

What are some examples of the various goals of disaster response? They range from utilizing scarce resources, to saving lives and protecting property, to securing politically viable decisions when a disaster occurs.

Why is there limited consensus among stakeholders on what constitutes a successful evacuation? There is limited consensus among various stakeholder groups as to what constitutes a successful evacuation.
What changes have been made to evacuation plans since Hurricane Katrina? Some evacuation issues have been considered and plans adjusted to one degree or another.
Are the changes made to evacuation plans proactive or reactive? These solutions are reactive responses to particular problems.

Do the changes made to evacuation plans ensure that new shortcomings will not be identified in the next event? No, these solutions are stop gaps that do little to ensure that new shortcomings will not be identified in the next event.
What is a good evacuation? A good evacuation is a concept that is not clearly defined in the proposal, but it is something that is explored and discussed.
Who decides what a good evacuation means? According to the proposal, it is not specified who decides what a good evacuation means.
How does the concept of a good evacuation change during an evacuation? The proposal suggests that the concept of a good evacuation may change during an evacuation.
What is the main goal of the Convergence HTF: RCN project? The main goal is to enhance small and mid-level farm viability.
What type of disciplines are being converged in the RCN project? The project is a convergence of various engineering, socio behavioral and humanistic disciplines.
Who is the PI (Principal Investigator) of the RCN project? The PI is Srinivasan.
What organization is funding the RCN project? The National Science Foundation is funding the project.
What is the purpose of the steering committee in the RCN project? Triantis is a member of the steering committee.

What types of technological innovations are expected to impact farming? New technological innovations ranging from assistive technology, precision agriculture, agricultural robots and sensor development for land and resource monitoring.
How will these technological innovations affect farming? They are expected to significantly impact the day-to-day operational ease and costs of farming.
What kind of benefits can farmers expect from these innovations? Significant impact on day-to-day operational ease and costs.

What disciplines should technological innovation research converge with? engineering, socio-behavioral, and humanistic disciplines such as human factors, health and safety, systems engineering and an integrated approach to sustainability in food and farming systems.

How should research be combined with other efforts to maximize the impact of technology in farming? Research should go hand-in-hand with extension, outreach, education, and farmer capacity-building efforts best achieved through a community participatory framework.
What is the goal of combining research with community participatory efforts? The goal is to maximize the impact and acceptance of technology in farming.
What is the main objective of the workshop? The objective of this workshop is to investigate and define theoretical foundations in systems engineering.
Who is the PI for the workshop? The PI for the workshop is Triantis.
What program is funding the workshop? The workshop is funded by the National Science Foundation, System Science Program.
What is the workshop's focus area? The workshop's focus area is a fundamental thrust area in systems engineering.

What will the workshop participants do to achieve the objective? The workshop participants will follow an approach that will address theoretical gaps in the literature, define an axiomatic framework, suggest what can be borrowed from other disciplines, investigate synergies among thrust areas and link the theoretical foundations to societal needs.
What kind of gaps will the workshop participants address in the literature? Theoretical gaps in the literature.
What will the workshop participants suggest? What can be borrowed from other disciplines.
What will the workshop participants investigate? Synergies among thrust areas.
What will the workshop participants link to societal needs? The theoretical foundations.
What can be done with the approach followed in this workshop if it is successful? The approach can be used and augmented to address additional thrust areas in the future.
What will be created if the approach is successful? Rigorous theoretical frameworks that can be tested with solid empirical fieldwork.
What will happen to the theoretical frameworks created? They will be tested with solid empirical fieldwork.
What is the focus of the research portfolio? The focus is on developing a sustainable and resilient water management system.
What geographic area is the research portfolio focused on? The Greater Washington, DC Metropolitan Area.
Who is the co-principal investigator of the research portfolio? Drs. (no specific names mentioned)
Who are the co-principal investigators for this project? Triantis (ISE), Murray-Tuite (CEE), Wolf (CPAP) and Wernstedt (School of Public and International Affairs) are the co-principal investigators.
Who is the principal investigator for this project? Dr. Moglen (CEE) is the principal investigator.
What is the focus of this project? The project aims to study a range of technical alternatives and institutional approaches for addressing future water challenges.
What is the name of the challenge that this project is a part of? This project is a part of the ICTAS Grand Challenge at Virginia Tech.
What is ICTAS? ICTAS stands for Institute for Critical Technology and Applied Science.
What kind of tools are available in the SPL portfolio? These options serve as a broad portfolio of tools for engineers, scientists, managers, and decision-makers.

Is the SPL portfolio focused on a single solution to water problems? The portfolio will not stake the solution to water problems on a single path, but will address these problems from different angles and across many scales.
Who can use the tools in the SPL portfolio? These options serve as a broad portfolio of tools for engineers, scientists, managers, and decision-makers.
What kind of solutions will result from this approach? Robust and resilient solutions

What is transforming the market that MCT is competing in? Economic and technological forces, including the increasing strength of digital media, the declining position of traditional news delivery systems, and the growth of complex search, editing, delivery and data mining technologies.
What is happening to traditional news delivery systems? Their position is declining.
What is driving the transformation of the market that MCT is competing in? Economic and technological forces.
What is growing in the market that MCT is competing in? Complex search, editing, delivery and data mining technologies.
What is changing in the economics and functionality of news agencies? The growth of complex search, editing, delivery and data mining technologies.

What is the goal of this project for MCT? The goal of this project is to help MCT understand its product/service processes, identify effective intervention strategies, and propose suitable change concepts to continuously reshape its business.

Why is MCT's business process agility important? MCT's business process agility is important to benefit from technological innovations and take advantage of new opportunities for increased productivity without compromising established sources of revenue.

What are the main areas of focus for this project? The main areas of focus for this project are understanding MCT's product/service processes, identifying effective intervention strategies, and proposing suitable change concepts.

What will happen if MCT's business processes are not reshaped? If MCT's business processes are not reshaped, it may miss out on opportunities for increased productivity and may compromise established sources of revenue.

What is the expected outcome of this project for MCT? The expected outcome of this project is that MCT will be best positioned to benefit from technological innovations and poised to take advantage of new opportunities for increased productivity.

What is the purpose of the research on road maintenance efficiency? The purpose of this research is to develop and implement a comprehensive framework that can measure the overall efficiency of road maintenance operations.
Who are the co-principal investigators of the research? Triantis is the co-principal investigator, working with Dr. de la Garza, the principal investigator.
What is the name of the funding agency for this research? The research is funded by the National Science Foundation, specifically through the CMMI program.

What challenges does the research aim to address in road maintenance operations? The research aims to address the challenge of measuring the efficiency of road maintenance operations, given multiple inputs and outputs that are incommensurate, and the impact of uncontrollable factors such as climate and traffic.
What methodology will be used in this research? The research will use and expand Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) to measure the efficiency of road maintenance operations.
What is the topic of the research led by Dr. Triantis? Traffic Congestion: Actions and Reactions
Who is the principal investigator for the research on Traffic Congestion? Triantis

Who is assisting Dr. Triantis in the research on Traffic Congestion? Dr. Triantis' collaborator is not explicitly mentioned, but another researcher is Dr. Triantis with Dr. [Name not mentioned in the text].

What are some of the factors being studied in the research on Traffic Congestion? climate, location, etc.

What does the interdisciplinary research aim to consider? The causes and ramifications of change altering our spatial and social world
What is the goal of designing social institutions in the research? To ameliorate traffic congestion

What is the main goal of the research proposed by the Office of Naval Research? The main goal is to develop system dynamics based cost models that will be complemented with efficiency optimization concepts and structures.
Who is the principal investigator for the research project? The principal investigator is Triantis.

What was the purpose of the research project? The purpose of the research project was to develop Total Quality Management (TQM) assessment and design guidelines to be used by the Navy to award contracts based on TQM criteria.
Who was the principal investigator of the research project? The principal investigator of the research project was Triantis.
Where was the research project based? The research project was based in Naval Warfare Assessment Center Corona, California and Naval Sea Systems Command Crystal City, VA.
What type of project was this? This was a research project.
What type of guidelines were developed? Total Quality Management (TQM) assessment and design guidelines were developed.
What is the LEAP-HI project about? The LEAP-HI project is about safety and learning from errors and near misses in the human-automation interaction of socio-technical infrastructure systems.
How does the LEAP-HI project relate to critical decision-making? The LEAP-HI project explores how technology impacts critical decision-making.
What is the focus of the LEAP-HI project in terms of cognitive biases? The LEAP-HI project focuses on how cognitive biases influence trust in automation.
What is the name of the professor leading the LEAP-HI project? The professor leading the LEAP-HI project is Kostas Triantis.
How much funding has the LEAP-HI project received from the National Science Foundation? The LEAP-HI project has received a four-year, $2 million grant from the National Science Foundation.

What is the role of a control center in a railway operation? Control centers serve as hubs of the newest rail technology and help ensure the smooth operation of train services, provide passengers with information, and resolve safety incidents.
What is another name for a control center in a railway operation? Also known as the 'nerve centers' of the rail network.
What is at the heart of every control center? The interface between human operators and technology.
What do control centers help ensure for train services? The smooth operation of train services.
What do control centers provide to passengers? Information.
What do people often believe about technology and its impact on efficiency, quality, safety, and cost? Technology alone is believed to improve efficiency, quality, safety, and cost.

What did train drivers in the earliest years of the railway industry rely on to perform their jobs safely? Train drivers in the earliest years of the railway industry relied heavily on their own perceptions and judgment.
Can technology also introduce errors and adverse events? Yes, technology may also introduce errors and adverse events.
What methods were used by train drivers in the past? Those methods were augmented with technology designed to work with a variety of modern driver systems.
What kind of technologies do train drivers encounter in the cab today? More automated technologies than ever before.
How have train driver systems changed over time? They have been augmented with technology designed to work with a variety of modern driver systems.
What is the purpose of the National Science Foundation award? The purpose of the National Science Foundation award is to fund research led by Kostas Triantis.
What field of study is Kostas Triantis a professor in? Kostas Triantis is a professor in the Grado Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering.
What type of tasks are being carried out by increasingly autonomous technologies? Safety-critical tasks are being carried out by increasingly autonomous technologies.
Who will be leading the research funded by the National Science Foundation award? Kostas Triantis will be leading the research funded by the National Science Foundation award.

What is still not fully understood about technology and its impacts? The impacts of technology and how decisions are best made between human operators and increasingly autonomous technologies are still not fully understood.
What disciplines will researchers be collaborating from for the project? decision theory, organizational theory, systems engineering and human factors engineering
Which European organizations are collaborating on the project? INFRABEL, Pro Rail, Network Rail, European Agency for Railways
Which country's organizations are also collaborating on the project? U.S.
What organizations are involved in this research? Union Pacific and the VOLPE Center are involved in this research.
What are the goals of this research? The goals of this research are to improve safety levels, staff wellbeing, and the smart use of automation in control rooms.
What role will automation play in the future? Automation will play an even more important role in the future than it does today.

What types of control room datasets and metrics are expected to emerge from the project? New and innovative types of control room datasets and metrics are expected to emerge from the interactions between researchers, data engineers, and other project collaborators.
Who received their Ph.D.? Roets received his Ph.D.
What will happen as a result of the interactions between researchers and data engineers? New and innovative types of control room datasets and metrics will emerge.
Where is the person from Ghent University located? Belgium

What does the person do for the research team? Analyzes new data
What committee does the person serve on? Ph.D.

What does a research committee member do? A research committee member contributes to various research journal papers and is the intermediary between INFRABEL and other European infrastructure providers.

What are the responsibilities of a research committee member? A research committee member contributes to various research journal papers that the research team works on, and is the intermediary between INFRABEL and other European infrastructure providers.

What are the tasks of a research committee member? A research committee member contributes to various research journal papers that the research team works on, and is the intermediary between INFRABEL and other European infrastructure providers.
What does the research committee member do for INFRABEL? The research committee member acts as an intermediary between INFRABEL and other European infrastructure providers.
What research does the committee member contribute to? The research committee member contributes to various research journal papers that the research team works on.
What is the name of the researcher mentioned in the text? Triantis

How long is the research project funded by the NSF grant? four years

What is the focus of the research project led by Triantis? how cognitive biases influence trust in automation and decisions to delegate tasks to automated technologies
What drives safe aviation in the airline industry? Air traffic flow management and collaborative decision-making drive safe aviation.
Why do pilots need to interact with automated systems manually? When circumstances dictate, pilots need to intervene and use systems manually.
What happens to a pilot's workload when they are using automated systems? Their monitoring workload becomes larger, which increases the probability of making an error.
What happened to the Boeing Max jet? The Boeing Max jet was involved in two fatal accidents in 2018 and 2019.
Why were the pilots unable to handle the automated system MCAS? The pilots were not trained on the new technology.
What was the result of the pilots not being able to override the MCAS system? There was a crash and loss of life.
When did the fatal accidents involving the Boeing Max jet occur? The accidents occurred in 2018 and 2019.
Why do controllers turn off automated systems during peak times? Regulating the transportation network becomes too complicated.
What happens when controllers turn off automated systems? The automated systems are turned off.
Who are the people that controllers often turn off automated systems for? Engineers, operators, and managers.
How do different controllers respond to scenarios? Different controllers respond to scenarios in a variety of ways.
Are there any differences in how younger and older controllers respond to scenarios? Yes, younger controllers tend to use the automated systems more than their senior colleagues.
Do all controllers use automated systems? No, younger controllers tend to use the automated systems more than their senior colleagues, suggesting that not all controllers use automated systems.
Why do transportation agencies want to use technology more? To achieve cost efficiency.
What factors do transportation agencies need to balance when deciding to use technology? Economic feasibility, safety, and workload demands placed on operators.
What are some of the benefits of using technology in transportation agencies? Cost efficiency.

Why is managing human-technology interaction critical in the healthcare sector? Managing human-technology interaction is critical in the healthcare sector because it affects situation awareness and mental workload, which are essential for effectively managing automated systems.
What can automated systems in operating rooms prevent? Automated systems in operating rooms have the potential to prevent many potentially harmful incidents.

What can happen if automated systems are poorly designed? Poorly designed automated systems may cause more incidents instead of preventing them, especially when automation reduces the human's role to being primarily supervisory.
What is the LEAP-HI grant? The LEAP-HI grant is the basis for further exploration of certain issues.

What type of grant has Triantis secured? A seed grant has been secured by Triantis.
What is the location of the collaboration with MEDSTAR and Carilion? The collaboration is taking place in Northern Virginia and Roanoke.

What kind of approach has the researcher been seeking? A more holistic approach.
What has the researcher's focus been over time? More and more multidisciplinary.
What are some of the challenges that SPL is trying to address? The integration of social networks and their behaviors with technological change is one of the challenges.
What is SPL trying to evaluate the impacts of? Technological investments.
What kind of problems is SPL trying to address with theoretical paradigms and modeling approaches? Complex societal problems.
What is SPL trying to see the relevance of? Specific theoretical paradigms and different modeling approaches.
What is multidisciplinary research? It involves doing research that combines different approaches from various disciplines.
How do different disciplines approach research? Different approaches pertain to different disciplines.
What is the relationship between theory and reality in research? Some of the theory actually relates to reality.
What areas of study will the project stimulate interest in? The sociotechnical domain area for students going into STEM-related fields.
How will the research findings be used? The research findings will be integrated into the curricula for undergraduate and graduate students.
What types of students will the project support? The project will support students going into STEM-related fields.

What is the main goal of the outreach activities mentioned on the SPL website? The overall goal is to reach out to a diverse group of students on the human-automation interface and the modeling of socio-technical systems.
What types of students are targeted by the outreach activities? Students going into STEM related fields.
What disciplines are stimulated by the outreach activities? LEAP HI related disciplines.
What are the main elements of the SPL plan? The principal elements of the plan include course development and implementation.

Production pressure is the organizational push to do more with less, which can quietly shift tradeoffs toward efficiency at the expense of safety — especially in fast-changing socio-technical environments. SPL's systematic review identifies production pressure as a widely recognized contributor to accidents. A key challenge is that empirical work often lacks consistent definitions and measures, motivating better monitoring approaches. Reference: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0925753522003848

SPL studies how people, automation, and organizations interact inside safety-critical, high-consequence systems — the kinds of systems that must work reliably in real time, such as rail traffic control. A core theme is turning dynamic safety concepts into measurable, computable models that help operators and leaders understand when performance is stable and when it is drifting toward unsafe conditions.

LEAP-HI is a $2 million NSF-funded research project to understand decision-making and safety in automated socio-technical infrastructure systems and to build a computable safe area of operation. It targets how mental workload and situational awareness change with automation, how teams detect and learn from errors and near misses, how trust and cognitive bias affect delegation to automation, and how workload, marginal safety, and economic boundaries define safe operating space.

The safe area of operation is a map of operating conditions where a system can run effectively without pushing people or technology beyond limits. LEAP-HI frames this safe region using interacting boundaries — especially workload and other performance limits — so decision makers can evaluate tradeoffs and avoid drifting into high-risk zones.

The workload boundary defines the practical upper limit to how much work a safety-critical system and its human operators can handle before performance degrades. SPL research operationalizes this by estimating workload boundaries in rail traffic control centers using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) and by accounting for differences in operating contexts — called performance environment heterogeneity. Ignoring context differences can overestimate targets and inadvertently push people toward unsafe workload levels. Reference: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0377221719303297

SPL uses large-scale operational microdata — including 410,269 controller-hour observations from real rail traffic control centers — combined with count models to study error patterns, system dynamics to capture feedback loops and fatigue and threshold effects, and DEA-based benchmarking enhanced with LSTM forecasting to support performance monitoring and decision-making. SPL also gathers qualitative data through interviews and on-site observations across shifts.

SPL research on automation and human error finds that errors increase with workload, and the relationship between automation reliance and errors is non-linear — an inverted U-shape. More automation is not automatically safer; performance depends on how much automation is used and under what workload conditions. Reference: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0925753524003655

Automation algorithms can influence phases of organizational learning beyond knowledge creation — including knowledge application and retention. In a study of U.S. passenger railroads adopting Positive Train Control (PTC), SPL research found support for hypotheses predicting faster learning from failures and longer retention of that learning.

SPL does not work only in rail and transportation. While rail and transportation are key application areas, SPL's approaches are designed for any socio-technical system where people, technology, and organizations interact — especially where safety, reliability, and performance matter. This includes healthcare, other critical infrastructure operations, and similarly complex high-stakes environments.

SPL is developing hands-on K-12 outreach experiences including STEaM Tech (https://www.spl.ise.vt.edu/outreach.html) that introduce students to systems thinking, decision-making, and human-automation interaction through interactive real-world transportation challenges.

For partner organizations, SPL research delivers improved operational performance — both safety and efficiency. SPL work clarifies when workload and automation configurations keep performance stable versus when they increase error risk, and translates those insights into actionable guidance for staffing, scheduling, tool and interface design, and escalation and coordination practices. SPL also uses DEA-based benchmarking to help partners quantify efficiency-safety tradeoffs, set realistic performance targets across different operating contexts, and monitor organizational pressures that can quietly erode safety margins while chasing productivity.
